description_long: This is a tall, slender coniferous tree with a straight, brown trunk and sparse, flat green branches arranged in tiers along its height. The branches are angular and stylized, with each layer becoming shorter toward the top, giving the tree a triangular silhouette. The foliage is dark green and appears to be made of broad, flat segments rather than individual needles, suggesting a stylized or low-poly design. There are no visible decorations, ornaments, or lights on the tree, and the top is pointed with no topper. The base of the tree is not visible, but the trunk extends straight down, indicating it is meant to stand upright. This tree resembles a natural evergreen, such as a pine or fir, and is suitable for use as a decorative element in urban parks or public spaces.
description: A tall, stylized evergreen tree with a brown trunk and sparse, flat green branches arranged in tiers. The tree is undecorated and has a pointed top.
description_view_0: The tree is seen from an angle showing the layered branches on the left side, with the trunk clearly visible and the foliage appearing dense at the lower half.
description_view_1: This view shows the tree from a side profile, highlighting the thinness of the trunk and the flat, angular arrangement of the branches.
description_view_2: The tree is viewed from the opposite side, with the branches appearing more symmetrical and the trunk centrally aligned.
description_view_3: This angle displays the tree with the branches fanning out more to the right, and the trunk is still visible but partially obscured by foliage.
